module1.clean_text:
Hospitalization report (immunoglobulin treatment)
The infant [PATIENT] aged [AGE] years and [AGE] originally from and residing in Saida, from a young 2nd degree consanguineous couple, followed at our level for the management of severe primary combined immunodeficiency type HLADR since [DATE] admitted today for treatment with immunoglobulin N 12. Main diagnosis: primary immunodeficiency PID type HLADR
Associated diagnoses: none
Date of diagnosis: [DATE] Age at diagnosis: [AGE] and a half [AGE] Arguments of the diagnosis:
Anamnestics:
-Consanguinity of the 2nd degree. Clinic:
-Repeated infections since the age of [AGE] -Infectious episode at the age of [AGE] impetiginized herpetic gingivostomatitis with whitlow on the thumb of the left hand with loss of substance. Paraclinic:
-Immune deficiency assessment: done on: [DATE] (attached): all the results are compatible with deficiency in HLA class 2 molecules (HLADR deficiency). IgG:13.2 g/l IGA:0.09 g/l IgM:1.02 g/l. Processing in progress:
Bactrim 20mg/kg/d
Itraconazol 5mg/kg/d
Zomax 01 DDP/ 1 day out of 2
Immunoglobulin treatment at a rate of 0.8mg1/kg/4h at the SAP each month. Last dose received: [DATE] history:
- [DATE] Appearance of a periungual lesion on the thumb of the left hand which prompted the parents to consult a private pediatrician who prescribed: Lexine syrup, fucidin ointment, Flammazine ointment,
- Last immunoglobulin dosage: [DATE] 13.74 g/l
CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION: Date [DATE] measurements:
Weight: 14kg (-0.6 DS) Height: 87 CM (-3DS). BMI: 18.5% (+2 SD) Overweight
General condition preserved, afebrile: T: 36.5° C, severe malnutrition,
Examination of the skin and appendages:
Presence of a lesion on the thumb of the left hand: erythema + desquamation, painless, red, normal temperature. Pleuropulmonary examination: Eupneic FR: 20 cycles/min, SaO2: 99% under AA, Auscultation clear. Cardiovascular examination: HR: 88 b/min, CRT < 3 sec, Auscultation: B1, B2 audible properties, absence of added noises
Abdominopelvic examination: Soft abdomen, no organomegaly, free hernial orifices, preserved transit
ENT: Clean throat, Absence of oral aphthosis, beginning of a yellowish discoloration of the teeth. Review of other devices: Is unremarkable
PARACLINICAL ASSESSMENT UPON ADMISSION: 04/07/2026
NFS: GB: 4650 element, PNN: 1020; lymphocyte: 2730
HB: 13.1g/dl; MCV: 84.9 fl ; CCMH: 34.6
PLQ: 302,000 el/mm 3. CRP: negative PCT: less than 0.5 VS: 53 mm/h (1st hour) 96 mm/h (2nd hour)
Ionogram: Na: 133.2mmol/l K: 3.84 mmol/l Cl: 104.1 mmol/l
Support and evolution:
Hospitalization
Conditioning + peripheral venous lines
Immunoglobulin 0.7g/kg Or 10g
Dietary interview
Psychological interview. The immunoglobulin treatment passed without incident, the child was discharged with a follow-up appointment for 04/13/2026
To collect / To do:
-Continue treatment: lexin syrup + Fucidin ointment + Flammazine ointment
-Increase in doses of bactrim (30mg/kg) + Itraconazole (10mg/kg) in curative dose; - Assessment of immunodeficiency in parents
-
Department resident: Dr Djoudi Department assistant: Dr Khelil

phenopacket.input:
Hospitalization report (immunoglobulin treatment)
The infant Abdoune Lyne Hasnia, aged 2 years and 2 months, originally from and residing in Saida, from a young 2nd degree consanguineous couple, followed at our level for the management of severe primary combined immunodeficiency type HLADR since June 2025, admitted today for treatment with immunoglobulin N 12. Main diagnosis: primary immunodeficiency PID type HLADR
Associated diagnoses: none
Date of diagnosis: 05/23/2025. Age at diagnosis: 15 and a half months. Arguments of the diagnosis:
Anamnestics:
-Consanguinity of the 2nd degree. Clinic:
-Repeated infections since the age of 12 months. -Infectious episode at the age of 17 months: impetiginized herpetic gingivostomatitis with whitlow on the thumb of the left hand with loss of substance. Paraclinic:
-Immune deficiency assessment: done on: 05/23/2025 (attached): all the results are compatible with deficiency in HLA class 2 molecules (HLADR deficiency). IgG:13.2 g/l IGA:0.09 g/l IgM:1.02 g/l. Processing in progress:
Bactrim 20mg/kg/d
Itraconazol 5mg/kg/d
Zomax 01 DDP/ 1 day out of 2
Immunoglobulin treatment at a rate of 0.8mg1/kg/4h at the SAP each month. Last dose received: 03/10/2026
Intermediate history:
- 03/30/2026: Appearance of a periungual lesion on the thumb of the left hand which prompted the parents to consult a private pediatrician who prescribed: Lexine syrup, fucidin ointment, Flammazine ointment,
- Last immunoglobulin dosage: 02/03/2026: 13.74 g/l
CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION: Date 04/07/2026
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 14kg (-0.6 DS) Height: 87 CM (-3DS). BMI: 18.5% (+2 SD) Overweight
General condition preserved, afebrile: T: 36.5° C, severe malnutrition,
Examination of the skin and appendages:
Presence of a lesion on the thumb of the left hand: erythema + desquamation, painless, red, normal temperature. Pleuropulmonary examination: Eupneic FR: 20 cycles/min, SaO2: 99% under AA, Auscultation clear. Cardiovascular examination: HR: 88 b/min, CRT < 3 sec, Auscultation: B1, B2 audible properties, absence of added noises
Abdominopelvic examination: Soft abdomen, no organomegaly, free hernial orifices, preserved transit
ENT: Clean throat, Absence of oral aphthosis, beginning of a yellowish discoloration of the teeth. Review of other devices: Is unremarkable
PARACLINICAL ASSESSMENT UPON ADMISSION: 04/07/2026
NFS: GB: 4650 element, PNN: 1020; lymphocyte: 2730
HB: 13.1g/dl; MCV: 84.9 fl ; CCMH: 34.6
PLQ: 302,000 el/mm 3. CRP: negative PCT: less than 0.5 VS: 53 mm/h (1st hour) 96 mm/h (2nd hour)
Ionogram: Na: 133.2mmol/l K: 3.84 mmol/l Cl: 104.1 mmol/l
Support and evolution:
Hospitalization
Conditioning + peripheral venous lines
Immunoglobulin 0.7g/kg Or 10g
Dietary interview
Psychological interview. The immunoglobulin treatment passed without incident, the child was discharged with a follow-up appointment for 04/13/2026
To collect / To do:
-Continue treatment: lexin syrup + Fucidin ointment + Flammazine ointment
-Increase in doses of bactrim (30mg/kg) + Itraconazole (10mg/kg) in curative dose; - Assessment of immunodeficiency in parents
-
Department resident: Dr Djoudi Department assistant: Dr Khelil
